rationale: Ansari, Mela, and Neslin (2008) is cited multiple times for concrete roles: evidence on channel migration/omnichannel behavior, heterogeneity in advertising response requiring individual-level panel data, and catalog carryover patterns. These references are used to justify the citing paper's core design choices (customer-level panel, multichannel sales modeling, and channel-based segmentation) and to interpret results. The citing paper explicitly states it further refines the omnichannel segment identified by Ansari et al., indicating a meaningful build-on relationship rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Ansari et al.'s channel migration and multichannel segmentation insights, then extends them by modeling multimedia advertising effects across multiple retailer-brands and two purchase channels. It also refines the prior omnichannel segment into more detailed brand-channel segments.
